title: Lip-sync fraud API — reference clips & threshold R&D
notes: Scores below are baselines for calibration; re-run scripts/update_lipsync_reference_data.py --measure to refresh after model/code changes.
SyncNet: lower min_dist is better; PASS when min_dist <= MIN_DIST_PASS and confidence >= CONFIDENCE_PASS.
MediaPipe: higher Pearson correlation (lip openness vs audio RMS) is better; PASS when correlation >= MEDIAPIPE_CORR_THRESHOLD.
Fusion LIPSYNC_FUSION=all requires BOTH pipelines to PASS. Official VGG example.avi should PASS with sane thresholds; random YouTube rips often score worse.

quick_copy_env_for_calibration.comment: Paste into .env then restart uvicorn — passes VGG example.avi under fusion=all; videoplayback.mp4 still FAIL both.

clips.description: Oxford VGG lipsync distribution sample; expect genuine A/V sync.
User test clip (often re-encoded / variable framing); may score poorly without looser thresholds.

calibrated_pass_vgg_fail_heavy_youtube_r.comment: Passes official example on fusion=all; keeps videoplayback failing on both signals. (Any row in threshold_sweep.presets_pass_vgg_only_all works; 6.58+ gives tiny slack above 6.575.)

lenient_fusion_any.comment: Either pipeline can save the verdict; use only after you trust each signal separately.
